channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math, according to the latest results of an exam known as the nation’s report card. The findings are yet another setback for U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats leave 'emergency' meeting vowing ‘street fight’ in response to Trump actions
item.description: House Democrats launched a multilateral effort Wednesday to combat the flood of executive actions from President Trump, vowing to punch back through legislation, lawsuits and a blitz of counter-messaging.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate advances Trump’s pick to lead the US Interior, Doug Burgum, to confirmation vote
item.description: Former North Dakota Gov. Doug Burgum previously told lawmakers that national security and the economy are his top priorities for leading the Interior Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans crafting Trump's big bill hit a familiar roadblock: How to pay for it
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — After fours years of criticizing growing government deficits under Joe Biden, Republicans now have a math problem of their own — how to offset the multitrillion-dollar cost of extending and expanding the tax cuts they passed in President Donald Trump’s first term.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump favorability higher than at start of first term: Poll
item.description: President Trump’s favorability rating at the start of his second term is significantly higher than it was when he first took office in January 2017, according to a Quinnipiac University poll released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bob Menendez sentenced to 11 years in prison following first-of-its-kind conviction
item.description: Former Sen. Bob Menendez (D-N.J.) was sentenced Wednesday to 11 years in prison for trading bribes for political favors.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is looking to boot transgender troops from the military. Here's why that's complicated
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has launched his second bid to oust all transgender troops from the military, and once again it will be headed to the courts to sort it out.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's EPA nominee stays vague on plans as senators press him on climate views
item.description: Lee Zeldin said Americans deserve a clean environment “without suffocating the economy” during his Senate confirmation hearing Thursday to lead the Environmental Protection Agency, a department likely to play a central role in President-elect Donald Trump’s pledge to slash federal regulations and pr
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act, plans to send illegal immigrants to Guantanamo
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's directing the opening of a detention center at Guantanamo Bay to hold up to 30,000 migrants who are living without legal permission in the United States. The U.S. military base has been used to house detainees from the U.S. war on terrorism.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard's views on Russia, Syria, Trump and government surveillance
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As an unconventional pick to lead the nation's intelligence service, Tulsi Gabbard is expected to face tough questions about her past comments on Syria, Russia, foreign surveillance and President Donald Trump when she goes before lawmakers at her confirmation hearing Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Democratic Party in crisis will elect its new chairman on Saturday
item.description: The Democratic National Committee will elect a new chair Saturday as it tries to guide Democrats through Republican Donald Trump’s second presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Center for Victims of Torture dramatically slashes workforce after Trump administration orders
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders forced many international humanitarian organizations, like St. Paul-based Center for Victims of Torture, to cut spending and furlough staff.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order to plan nation's 250th anniversary celebration, punish those who vandalize statues
item.description: President Donald Trump is making plans to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American independence and create a new national sculpture garden while reviving efforts to harshly punish those who vandalize or destroy existing statues and monuments.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate Judiciary advances Bondi AG nomination on party-line vote
item.description: President Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, Pam Bondi, on Wednesday advanced out of the Senate Judiciary Committee on a strict party-line vote, setting the stage for a vote on the Senate floor.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Frey formally launches bid for third term as Minneapolis mayor
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said Wednesday he’ll seek a third — and final — term as mayor to continue his administration’s work overhauling the police department and building more affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Defense secretary pulls Trump critic Gen. Milley's security clearance and protective detail
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pulling the security protections and clearance of retired Joint Chiefs chairman Gen. Mark Milley.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Why members of two of EPA's influential science advisory committees were let go
item.description: Acting EPA Administrator James Payne sent an email to members of the agency's Science Advisory Board and the Clean Air Scientific Advisory Committee informing them that both are being "reset.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, MPR News

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails allegedly targeting GOP politicians
item.description: Officers said they found a folding knife “as well as two destructive devices from the inside pockets of ENGLISH’s jacket” when they searched Ryan English.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Karoline Leavitt, the youngest White House press secretary, makes her debut in the briefing room
item.description: The youngest person to serve as White House press secretary has made her debut in the briefing room, promising to hold briefings often and saying President Donald Trump also will be available to reporters for questions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Doctors opposing RFK Jr. rally in the lead-up to his confirmation
item.description: Many health professionals are lining up against Trump's pick for health secretary. They say his anti-vaccine views could cost lives. Some of his supporters embrace his stance.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: K-12 education and the Minnesota Legislature: 5 things to watch this session
item.description: Even as Minnesota lawmakers struggle to start their new session, education committee leaders in the House and Senate are quietly starting to set budget and policy priorities. Here’s a look at what might be coming.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: News for Trump: Denmark's last envoy to Greenland says U.K. may have first dibs
item.description: Denmark's last minister for Greenland says Britain once claimed right of first refusal over the territory — because of its proximity to Canada, which Britain once ruled.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer, MPR News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
